title: A Family's Decision to Self-Deport to Mexico Amidst ICE Threats
content:
When Irvi arrived in Oaxaca, Mexico, he was initially enchanted by the clear blue skies unmarred by skyscrapers, but he also felt deeply unnerved. Having spent years navigating New York City without a map, he now struggled to adapt to working long hours on his family's farm for barely any money. He had chosen to self-deport after being overwhelmed by the constant threat of armed immigration agents dragging him away from his family in the United States. It didn't help that his relatives constantly worried that his American wife, Rachel, and their two daughters might never actually join him in Mexico. They simply couldn’t believe that United States citizens would willingly trade the privileges of their home country just to be with him.

However, Rachel never once considered abandoning Irvi, feeling it was finally her turn to sacrifice to keep their family together. Without Irvi in New York, their daughters struggled emotionally, while Rachel worked to secure a poorly paid teaching job and a private school in Oaxaca. As immigration raids intensified downtown, Rachel quickly dismantled their New York lives and packed a trailer with their remaining belongings. Although her father was heartbroken and disappointed in the country's immigration policies, he supported their difficult decision to self-deport. Ultimately, the family traded the comforts of their old life for the relief of finally being out of the United States.
